Name:
George Miller
aka. MC George
Notes:
(??/??/2008) (George produces his first beat in GarageBand)
(??/??/2010) (George begins performing with Beats N' Miso)
Leak Date:
This era contains all of Joji's music before he started making music with the 
Pink Guy character. He first went by "MC George" in the video "Greatest Song Ever" before he later performed with the rap group Beats N' Baby Miso, which was when he started going by "MC Rukuss". In interviews, he stated that he started creating his first songs when he was 16, which would've been in 2008. He only ever released one song under the "MC George" name. He would also be in "BUSHIDO", which was uploaded to his friend's channel.

He stated that he whispered real low when recording the "A Milli" loop.

(??/??/2010) (George stops performing with Beats N' Miso)
(??/??/2013) (George privates all videos on the "RukussMC" channel)
Leak Date: Sometime between early to mid 2010, George would go under the alias of "MC Rukuss". He created a YouTube channel under that name and uploaded several skits and songs. During that time, he briefly joined a local Hip-Hop group named "Beats N' Miso" and performed with them throughout 2010. Between 2010 and 2011, he changed his alias to "Russ Rukuss". In 2013, he privated all videos on the "RukussMC" YouTube channel.

Leak Date: Two singles released a year later in 2015 with an announcement for "Chloe Burbank Volume 1" on social media. Joji began releasing more tracks on SoundCloud throughout the following years before signing to 88rising in hopes of releasing the project, but he was unfortunately met with sample clearance issues that he'd complain about on Twitter, and ultimately decided the decision to scrap the project, and began converting the project into a smaller EP.

(prod. Joji)
aka. foie, foie <bump>, Where'd You Go
Notes:
OG Project Filename: pills 2.gbproj
OG Filename: pills 2 - 2_24_16, 12.09.aif
OG Filename (Metadata): pills 2 - 2:24:16, 12.09 AM
Released on Joji’s SoundCloud account with vocals from "Juicy" by Biggie Smalls
over the beat. The song was not intended for the Chloe Burbank Volume 1 project,
as signified by the tags #not #chloe on the upload. The title "foie" is French for "liver"
which correlates with the cover art uploaded with the song, as it's a can of Cod Liver.
Joji later repurposed the beat for In Tongues and made it sound practically brand new.
The later version of "Pills" on In Tongues still uses many of the same sounds as "foie", as well as the recognizable "Finn" sample, which is a clip of BMO from Adventure Time.

(??/??/2017) (Chloe Burbank is scrapped and tracks from the project are selected to be exclusively released on WAV Media as a seperate project and as promotion for In Tongues)
(10/27/2017) (Lost Instruments is exclusively released on WAV Media)
Leak Date: “Lost Instruments” is a small collection of unreleased songs that was made in anticipation of Joji's debut EP "In Tongues". It was originally able to be streamed on WAV Media, a streaming platform intended to “give artists the support they need to succeed, while giving fans access to unique experiences they can’t get anywhere else” However, on December 22nd 2019, WAV Media was shut down, which meant that "Lost Instruments" was also taken down alongside the site.

Name: In Tongues

(??/??/2017) (Chloe Burbank is scrapped)
(10/27/2017) (Lost Instruments is released as promotion for In Tongues)
(11/03/2017) (In Tongues is released)
Leak Date: After scrapping most of "Chloe Burbank Volume 1", Joji began to work on converting the project into an EP for the following months, eventually becoming "In Tongues". Some reminants of the "Chloe Burbank Volume 1" project can be found throughout the EP, as "Pills" actually interpolates "Thom" from the previous project.

Name:
✨ Window (Actress Remix)
(prod. Actress)
Notes:
Remix of "Window" that Actress meant to submit. The snippets were leaked on November 18th, 2021 by Datura on LEAKED.CX. The "remix" on the deluxe version is actually a demo of his own song "Fire and Light", which would release 2 years later on his album "Karma & Desire". His request to resubmit the file was rejected by 88rising so they could ensure that the deluxe would release on Valentine’s Day.
The full remix was leaked by Jae on March 29th, 2026.

Name:
⭐️ WHAT ABOUT US?
(prod. Joji)
Notes:
Pt. 2 of "Casser". First heard in a leaked Discord Call with skyupsahl. Later leaked on DBREE by someone who privately bought the song and didn't have enough space on their phone, so they uploaded it there thinking it wasn't public. (LOL)
Samples a recording of Joji harmonizing in the bathroom, and there's a short rap-like verse with a beat switch at the end, which sounds very similar to the end of "Demons". The song that's heard in the intro is "Tennessee Waltz" by Patti Page.
"Casser" could be a nod to Brian Cassar, who was a British singer and guitarist known as Casey Jones. He led the first notable beat group Cass and the Cassanovas, who were early rivals of The Beatles in the city. His surname was also often misspelled as Casser.

Name: Nectar
Notes:
(10/26/2018) (BALLADS 1 is released)
(08/25/2020) (Nectar is finished)
(09/25/2020) (Nectar is released)
Leak Date:
After the release of the "BALLADS 1" project, Joji would begin working with more and more producers and writers, and eventually leaving behind the lofi & trap sounds that many of us became used to over the years. There was lots of marketing in between the releases, which made the project very memorable as well. The project was announced on April 17th 2020, and was planned for release on July 10th 2020. However, on June 12th 2020, Joji announced that the album had been delayed to September 25th, 2020, citing the COVID-19 pandemic.
A month after the release Joji would do a paid virtual live stream titled "The Extravaganza", with live perfomances and comedic skits.

Notes:
OG Filename: Joji x Daniel Wilson - Like It (08.18.20).mp3
OG Filename (2): Like It - Joji - FR Rough Mix 1.1 - No Melodyne.mp3
Two years after snippets for the song first leaked on October 11th, 2021, it was leaked after a successful groupbuy for $500. The price was originally $250, but someone was trying to vault the song for themselves to resell later on. The song features vocals from Daniel Wilson, who has written a lot of songs for Joji such as "Run" and "Sanctuary". On February 27th, 2025, another file for the song leaked without any quality difference.
Both flies are encoded by iTunes 12.6.2.20, which released on July 19th, 2017.

Name:
✨ Linden Jay - Keep Me Sleeping [V1]
(prod. Josh Taffel & Linden Jay)
aka. Like You Do
Notes:
OG Filename: 2. KEEP ME SLEEPING.wav
Four years before the release of Nectar, Linden Jay went to the studio one night with Josh Taffel and produced a beat he titled "Keep Me Sleeping", which he later decided to put on a compilation tape with other beats. He loved the instrumental but wished it had vocals. Years later he sent the song too Kurtis McKenzie, who wrote "Like You Do".

Name: SMITHEREENS
Notes:
(09/25/2020) (Nectar is released)
(11/04/2022) (SMITHEREENS is released)
Leak Date: After the release of "Nectar" and after the "Nectar: The Finale" tour had concluded, there was hiatus from Joji for six months before the release of the lead single "Glimpse of Us" which leaked days prior to the song's announcement, with the "SMITHEREENS" album releasing a few months later. The project has two discs, the first disc includes songs that Joji only wrote on and sang, but the second disc starts off with "NIGHT RIDER" which switches up the flow of the project, going from melancholic ballads to a trap beat with electronic-like elements. Sadly, the project continues with more songs that Joji only wrote on and sang like "1AM FREESTYLE", with the exception of "YUKON (INTERLUDE)".

Notes:
(11/04/2022) (SMITHEREENS is released)
(??/??/2025) (Joji leaves the 88rising label and forms Palace Creek)
Leak Date: Hours after the release of "SMITHEREENS", Joji announced another tour with the name "SMITHEREENS: OBLIVION" a small tour with only three dates. But before that tour even finished, Joji announced another tour with the name "PANDEMONIUM", which was finished earlier than planned due to health reasons. During this time, it is possible Joji was making another project under 88rising, but was ultimately scrapped once he left the label.

Notes: On Janurary 26th 2023 Joji's engineer Frankie Ramirez teased on Instagram that he mixed a couple things for Joji, stating "this just the precum y'all ain't seen nuttin yet".
